title: How to Make Moroccan Preserved Lemons with Sea Salt
description: Moroccan preserved lemons are so easy to make! This is the best way to preserve lemons, and the whole lemon is used. You only need two ingredients, lemons and sea salt.

ingredients.item: g fresh lemons (about 4 to 5 small lemons)

steps: Wash all of the lemons, and weigh out the salt in a bowl.
Cut the ends off the lemons, then starting at one end, cut each lemon into quarters, without cutting all the way through.
Over a large bowl, fill the crevices of the lemons with copious amounts of salt until all the salt is used up.
Start packing the whole lemons into a clean jar, wedging them in as tightly as possible, and lemon juice will be released in the process.
Once the jar is full make sure all the lemons are submerged in lemon juice; if you need to, juice the extra lemon and add the juice to the jar.
Place a lid on the jar and allow the lemons to cure at room temperature for 4 weeks.
Once opened, store in the fridge indefinitely; rinse off any excess salt and brine before using the preserved lemons in recipes.
